reasons: Esmeralda reduces the value of Phoveus's shield-supported close fights.
She can convert nearby enemy shield value into her own survivability during extended skirmishes.

proof.summary: Phoveus wants shield-supported close fights, while Esmeralda can reduce the value of that shield and turn it into her own survivability.
worksBestWhen: Esmeralda fights Phoveus in extended skirmishes.
She avoids being bursted before her shield cycle starts.
failureCases: Phoveus bursts or controls Esmeralda before she can sustain.
Esmeralda is kited before reaching him.
Phoveus's team collapses faster than Esmeralda can drain shield value.

reasons: Valir can reset spacing against Phoveus's close-range pressure.
Pushback and slows reduce Phoveus's ability to stay on priority targets.

proof.summary: Phoveus wants to force close fights, while Valir can reset spacing and make his engage harder to complete.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves peel for Phoveus's real entry.
Team backs away instead of fighting in Phoveus's range.
failureCases: Phoveus enters after Valir uses peel.

reasons: Lunox can pressure durable fighters with high magic burst and HP-sensitive damage.
Her defensive timing can help her avoid Phoveus's commitment window.

proof.summary: Phoveus wants to survive and keep fighting, while Lunox can punish him with burst before he gets extended uptime.
worksBestWhen: She saves defensive timing for Phoveus's engage.
failureCases: Phoveus catches Lunox after her defensive tools.
Lunox is forced to use burst on another target.

reasons: Alpha can match fighter trades with sustain and true damage.
His kit is suited for extended melee fights against Phoveus rather than fragile all-in burst.

proof.summary: Phoveus wants durable close-range skirmishes, while Alpha can answer with sustain, control, and true-damage pressure.
worksBestWhen: Alpha avoids being isolated before sustain starts.
failureCases: Alpha misses control or sustain timing.

reasons: X.Borg's Firaga Armor helps him avoid being instantly punished in Phoveus's close-range fights.
His true-damage pressure can wear down durable fighters in extended fights.

proof.summary: Phoveus wants to force close-range durability fights, while X.Borg can survive burst windows and answer with true-damage pressure.
worksBestWhen: X.Borg keeps Firaga Armor before committing.
He kites Phoveus before using ultimate.
failureCases: X.Borg loses armor before the fight.
X.Borg dives into too much control.
